$comment: Machine-readable Power BI formatting inventory for the Tableau->Power BI migration tool. Aggregates are corpus-grounded; curated layers are flagged.

valueGrammar.note: Power BI serializes formatting values in THREE distinct encodings depending on the layer. A migration tool MUST emit the right one per target.

literalSyntax.string: single-quoted inside the value string, e.g. "'Top'"

colorForms.themeTintShade: Percent in [-1,1]; negative = darker (shade), positive = lighter (tint); 0 = exact palette color

themeVisualStyles.note: Same conceptual property as visualContainerObject but different serialization: plain values + {solid:{color:'#hex'}}. fontSize is a plain string of points.

richTextTextStyle.note: Rich text is a third encoding again; fontSize carries a 'pt' unit and color is a plain hex string.

comparisonKindEnum.note: Observed in Conditional.Cases Comparison; mapping is the documented DataViewObjects ComparisonKind (inferred from PBI conventions, verify per case).

themeSchema.note: Report theme JSON. Base themes (BaseThemes/) carry the full schema; custom themes (RegisteredResources/) and built-in named themes (BuiltInThemes/) may be partial (colors only). All keys below were observed in the corpus unless marked.

dataColors.role: ordered categorical accent palette; index N assigned to Nth series/category; wraps when exhausted
dataColors.tableau: Tableau discrete color palette (Preferences.tps categorical) + Color on Marks

good.tableau: no native; build via calc + color

null.role: color for null values in a color scale

textClasses.note: 12 documented classes exist; corpus base themes serialize these 4 primary ones (others inherit).

visualStyles.tableau: no direct equivalent (Tableau lacks per-viz-type style defaults)

textClassFullList.note: Documented text-class names (schema/docs); corpus serializes the starred ones.

precedenceModel.note: Resolution order for a single property when multiple layers touch it (lowest to highest priority).

order.example: custom dataColors[] override the base palette
this bar chart's labels.color set to '#FFFFFF'

order.where: object instance with a selector (metadata / dataScope / dataWildcard)
order.example: dataPoint.fill with selector {dataScope:...} colors one category; Conditional/fillRule overrides by data value

notes: Higher rank wins. A per-visual explicit property (4) beats theme visualStyles (3), which beats the theme palette/structural default (1-2).
Within a visual, a selector-scoped instance (5) overrides the default (no-selector) instance of the same object/property for the matching scope.
ThemeDataColor refs (ColorId/Percent) are resolved against the WINNING palette after theme precedence (1-2) is applied, so re-theming recolors these automatically.

Literal hex values are frozen at author time and do NOT follow the theme; ThemeDataColor refs DO. This distinction is critical when migrating: Tableau colors are literal, so importing them yields literal hex (theme-independent) unless deliberately re-bound to ThemeDataColor.

categoryToTableau.color: Color on Marks card / color legend / Format shading
categoryToTableau.conditional: Color/label encoding driven by a measure (table-calc/CF) on the Marks card

The examples in this file have been built to show the features listed below and as a result might not be the best example of how to generally do things in Power BI.

If a preview feature does not work, be sure to enable it in settings.
Bookmark - Smart Narratives: Q&A now supports arithmetic operations
Bookmark - Data point rectangle select for additional charts
Bookmark - General visual option to maintain layer order
Bookmark - Total labels for stacked visuals

exampleValues.value: Click on the bubbles to see a visual with more information.
Net sales is the sum of a company''s gross sales minus its returns, allowances, and discounts.
The buyer returned an item within a certain number of days for a full refund.
Number of units returned for a full refund.
Is a determination of what is going to happen in the future by analyzing what happened in the past and what is going on now.
Interact with the variable as a slicer, and visualize and quantify different key values.

Financial benefit realized when revenue generated from a business activity exceeds the expenses and costs involved based in the "What if" parameter (The "What If" parameter must be a less porcentage of the "Retur Rate Porcentage" to display a Value.

Probability that a transaction that contains the items on the left hand side of the rule also contains the item on the right hand side.The higher the confidence, the greater the likelihood that the item on the right hand side will be purchased
